overview: This domain workflow pack defines the core processes for the AutoForge Shop e-commerce platform. It covers the customer journey from product discovery (browsing) through to order placement and viewing historical records. Key focus areas include inventory management, price validation, and robust checkout handling.

workflows.name: Product Browsing
workflows.description: The customer views the catalog, searching or filtering products to find items of interest.
steps: Customer lands on the catalog page.
System displays product listings (Name, Price, Image).
Customer applies filters (Category, Price Range).
System updates the displayed product list based on criteria.
exceptions: EX-001: Out of Stock (If a product is filtered/displayed but is out of stock, it must be flagged.)

workflows.name: Product Detail View
workflows.description: The customer views comprehensive details for a single product and prepares to add it to the cart.
steps: Customer selects a product from the catalog.
System displays full description, SKU, and current stock level.
Customer selects desired quantity.
System validates stock availability (BR-001).
Customer adds the item to the shopping cart.
exceptions: EX-001: Out of Stock (System prevents adding item if quantity exceeds available stock.)

workflows.name: Shopping Cart Management
workflows.description: The customer reviews, modifies, and calculates the total cost of items in the cart.
steps: Customer views the cart summary (Product, Quantity, Unit Price, Subtotal).
Customer adjusts quantity (AC-006).
System recalculates the subtotal and cart total instantly.
Customer removes items (AC-007).
exceptions: EX-003: Invalid Quantity (System rejects non-numeric or zero quantities.)

workflows.name: Checkout Process
workflows.description: The customer provides necessary shipping and billing information and reviews the final order summary.
steps: Customer initiates checkout.
System collects/confirms shipping and billing addresses (AC-008).
Customer applies discount codes (if applicable).
System calculates final total (including taxes/shipping) (AC-009).
Customer reviews the final summary and proceeds to payment.
exceptions: EX-003: Validation Error (System prompts user to correct invalid address or required fields.)

workflows.name: Order Placement
workflows.description: The system finalizes the transaction, processes payment, and confirms the order.
steps: System validates final inventory and pricing (BR-001, BR-002).
System reserves inventory (BR-004).
Customer submits payment details.
System attempts payment authorization (Payment Gateway).
If successful: System creates unique Order ID (AC-010), updates status to 'Processing', and records order details (AC-011).
If failed: System handles payment failure (EX-002) and prompts user to retry.
exceptions: EX-002: Payment Failure (System must not finalize the order and must release reserved inventory.)

workflows.name: Order History Viewing
workflows.description: The logged-in customer views a list of past orders and their detailed status.
steps: Customer navigates to 'My Orders'.
System displays list of past orders (ID, Date, Status) (AC-012).
Customer selects a specific Order ID.
System displays the detailed order summary (items, final price, shipping address) (AC-013).

business_rules.title: Stock Availability Check
business_rules.description: The system must validate that the requested quantity of every item in the cart is less than or equal to the current available stock before checkout and order placement.

business_rules.title: Pricing Integrity Validation
business_rules.description: The final price calculation (including discounts and taxes) must be validated against the current product catalog price and must not trust the price submitted by the client/frontend.

business_rules.title: Order Immutability
business_rules.description: Once an order is placed and confirmed, the order record (items, quantities, final price) must be treated as immutable, even if the product catalog price changes later.

business_rules.title: Inventory Reservation
business_rules.description: Upon successful checkout submission, the system must immediately reserve or deduct the required stock quantity for the items in the order to prevent overselling.

business_rules.title: Checkout Data Validation
business_rules.description: The system must enforce validation rules on all required fields (shipping address, billing address, contact information) before allowing order submission.

domain_entities.name: Product
domain_entities.description: Represents an item available for sale in the catalog.

domain_entities.name: Shopping Cart
domain_entities.description: A temporary collection of products selected by the customer before checkout.

domain_entities.name: Order
domain_entities.description: The final, confirmed record of a transaction.

domain_entities.name: Customer
domain_entities.description: The user profile containing personal and shipping information.

exceptions.name: Out of Stock
exceptions.description: A product is requested or added to the cart, but the current stock quantity is zero or insufficient.
exceptions.handling_rule: The system must display an 'Out of Stock' message and prevent the item from being added to the cart or ordered.

exceptions.name: Payment Failure
exceptions.description: The payment gateway rejects the transaction (e.g., insufficient funds, expired card).
exceptions.handling_rule: The order must not be created. Any reserved inventory must be immediately released back into the available stock count. The user must be prompted to correct payment details.

exceptions.name: Validation Error
exceptions.description: User input fails to meet required format or business rules (e.g., invalid email, non-numeric quantity, missing address field).
exceptions.handling_rule: The system must provide clear, field-specific error messages and prevent progression to the next step in the workflow.

assumptions: Users access the system through a web browser.
Admin product management can be added after MVP.
constraints: The first version focuses on catalog, cart, checkout, and orders.

# Appendix A — Production E-commerce Workflow Summary

## A.1 End-to-End Customer Purchase Flow

```text
1. User lands on homepage.
2. User searches or browses categories.
3. User opens product detail page.
4. User selects variant.
5. User adds item to cart.
6. System validates product and price.
7. User starts checkout.
8. System creates checkout session.
9. User enters shipping address.
10. System calculates shipping and tax.
11. User selects payment method.
12. System creates payment intent.
13. User confirms payment.
14. Payment provider authorizes/captures payment.
15. System creates order.
16. System reserves/deducts inventory.
17. System sends confirmation email.
18. Warehouse fulfills order.
19. Shipping provider delivers package.
20. Customer receives order.
21. Customer may review, return, or reorder.

tices

* Use clear domain boundaries: catalog, cart, checkout, payment, order, inventory, fulfillment.
* Maintain immutable order records after purchase.
* Use idempotency keys for payment and order APIs.
* Use event logs for critical business transitions.
* Validate prices and inventory at checkout, not only when adding to cart.
* Separate user-facing product data from internal inventory data.
* Use asynchronous processing for emails, invoices, notifications, fulfillment, analytics.
* Maintain audit logs for admin actions.
* Use secure defaults for authentication, authorization, payments, and APIs.

### Common Mistakes

* Treating the cart as a final order.
* Not locking or reserving inventory during checkout.
* Trusting frontend price values.
* Storing payment card data unnecessarily.
* Mixing product catalog logic with order logic.
* Not handling duplicate webhooks.
* Not supporting p

es

| Edge Case                     | Handling                         |
| ----------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| Partial shipment              | Track shipment per order item    |
| Partial refund                | Refund specific amount or item   |
| Payment pending too long      | Expire order or retry            |
| Duplicate webhook             | Idempotent status update         |
| Order cancelled after payment | Refund automatically or manually |
| Product no longer exists      | Use order snapshot               |
| Multi-currency order          | Store currency and exchange rate |
| Marketplace order             | Split by seller internally       |

## Best Practices

* Use state machines for status transitions.
* Store provider references for payments and shipping.
* Maintain order event history.
* Make fulfillment operations auditable.
* Notify user

|
| Checkout           | Collects shipping, billing, payment, and confirmation details               |
| Payment Processing | Handles card payments, wallets, bank transfers, refunds, disputes           |
| Order Management   | Tracks the full order lifecycle                                             |
| Inventory          | Tracks stock availability across warehouses and sales channels              |
| Fulfillment        | Handles picking, packing, shipping, delivery, and returns                   |
| Customer Accounts  | Stores profiles, addresses, preferences, order history                      |
| Admin Panel        | Allows business users to manage catalog, orders, users, promotions          |
| Analytics          | Tracks revenue, conversion, retention, customer behavior                    |
| Security           | Protects data, payments, authentication, A
